channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.
item.content:encoded:
Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: This lab that's determined to discover new drugs isn't where you might expect
item.description: A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.
item.content:encoded:
A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: An orthopedic surgeon explains the hand injury that has many MLB players on the bench
item.description: Baseball hitters are on a quest for power. But that quest comes at a cost. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to orthopedic surgeon Dr. Thomas DiLiberti about baseball players suffering hamate injuries.
item.content:encoded: Baseball hitters are on a quest for power. But that quest comes at a cost. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to orthopedic surgeon Dr. Thomas DiLiberti about baseball players suffering hamate injuries.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: She cared for her mother for 14 years. She says she'd do it all over again
item.description: Kathy Barnes-Lou cared for her mother for 14 years before her death. She learned that caregiving can bring life's purpose into focus, even as it grinds you down.
item.content:encoded: Kathy Barnes-Lou cared for her mother for 14 years before her death. She learned that caregiving can bring life's purpose into focus, even as it grinds you down.
item.dc:creator: Kat McGowan

item.title: Democrats who won big in last November's general election are grappling with reality
item.description: Some Democrats who were swept into office last November are grappling with the reality of governing. The new leader of Pennsylvania's Lehigh County says urgency is needed.
item.content:encoded: Some Democrats who were swept into office last November are grappling with the reality of governing. The new leader of Pennsylvania's Lehigh County says urgency is needed.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: The Iran war is impacting the global economy, and Asia is particularly vulnerable
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator for the Financial Times, about how the war on Iran is effecting the global economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator for the Financial Times, about how the war on Iran is effecting the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.
item.content:encoded: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Politics chat: Trump's mixed messages on the Iran war, the latest on DHS funding
item.description: We look at President Trump's mixed messages on the war with Iran, plus the latest on Department of Homeland Security funding, which Congress has frozen over his immigration enforcement policies.
item.content:encoded: We look at President Trump's mixed messages on the war with Iran, plus the latest on Department of Homeland Security funding, which Congress has frozen over his immigration enforcement policies.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
item.description: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.
item.content:encoded: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: The U.S. is a big oil exporter. So why does it import most of the oil it consumes?
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Texas engineering professor Hugh Daigle about why the U.S. imports most of the oil it consumes despite being one of the world's largest oil exporters.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to University of Texas engineering professor Hugh Daigle about why the U.S. imports most of the oil it consumes despite being one of the world's largest oil exporters.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe
